name: List
description: A linear collection of elements with a defined order. Often used to return a sequence of results such as filtered or sorted items.

name: Set
description: An unordered collection of unique elements. Useful for returning deduplicated data or results from set operations.

name: Map
description: A collection of key-value pairs, typically used to return associations, such as frequencies, groupings, or mappings.

name: Tuple
description: A fixed-size ordered collection of heterogeneous values. Often used to return multiple related values as a single result.

name: Matrix
description: A two-dimensional array structure. Suitable for returning grid-like data such as tables, image pixels, or graph representations.

name: Tree
description: A hierarchical data structure consisting of nodes, typically used for returning structured relationships such as parsed expressions or hierarchical configurations.

name: Graph
description: A structure consisting of nodes and edges. The output may be in the form of adjacency lists or matrices, suitable for network and connectivity data.

name: Struct
description: A user-defined data structure that groups related fields together. Often used to return complex structured results with named fields.

name: String
description: A sequence of characters. Commonly used to return serialized data, formatted messages, text responses, or paths.

name: JSONObject
description: A structured JSON object consisting of nested key-value mappings. Often used in web APIs and data interchange scenarios as a rich, self-describing output format.

name: Boolean
description: A binary value indicating true or false. Typically used as the result of a condition, check, or predicate function.

name: Integer
description: A numeric value used to return counts, computed values, indices, or rankings.

name: Float
description: A numeric value with decimals, commonly used for returning averages, ratios, or other computations involving fractions.
